# Chunk Reference: All R Markdown Chunk Types {#chunk_reference}

```{r chunkref-setup, include = FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ SETUP CHUNK                                                     │
# │ Purpose: Load packages, source shared setup, set chapter-wide  │
# │          knitr options. Always use include=FALSE.               │
# │ Options: include = FALSE                                        │
# │ Notes: Every chapter needs this. common.R sets global defaults  │
# │        (comment = "#>", collapse = TRUE). You can override here.│
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
source("common.R")
library(tidyverse)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(DT)
library(knitr)

if (!require("tweetrmd")) devtools::install_github("gadenbuie/tweetrmd")
library(tweetrmd)
library(vembedr)

ds4p_urls <- read.csv("./metadata/ds4p_urls.csv")
```

This page is a reference catalog of **every type of R Markdown code chunk** used in this course. Copy the patterns you need into your own chapters. Each chunk type is annotated with its purpose, key options, and when to use it.


## Setup and Configuration Chunks

### Standard Setup

Every chapter starts with a setup chunk that sources `common.R` and loads needed packages:

````markdown
```{r chapterXX-setup, include = FALSE}`r ''`
source("common.R")
library(tidyverse)
ds4p_urls <- read.csv("./metadata/ds4p_urls.csv")
```
````

### Chapter-Wide Default Override

Labs often override the global default so student code chunks don't auto-run:

````markdown
```{r labXX-setup, include = FALSE}`r ''`
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```
````

Or force everything to run (useful for lab answer keys):

````markdown
```{r labXX-setup, include = FALSE}`r ''`
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = TRUE)
```
````


## Package Loading

```{r chunkref-packages, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ PACKAGE LOADING CHUNK                                           │
# │ Purpose: Install-if-needed and load packages.                  │
# │ Options: message=FALSE, warning=FALSE                           │
# │ Notes: Suppresses startup messages (tidyverse logo, etc.)      │
# │        The if(!require()) pattern auto-installs missing pkgs.  │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
if (!require("palmerpenguins")) install.packages("palmerpenguins")
library(palmerpenguins)

if (!require("tidyverse")) install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
```


## Data Loading and Glimpse

```{r chunkref-data-glimpse, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ DATA LOADING / GLIMPSE CHUNK                                    │
# │ Purpose: Load data and show structure to orient the reader.     │
# │ Options: echo=TRUE (show the code), message=FALSE               │
# │ Notes: glimpse() is preferred over str() for tidyverse style.  │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
glimpse(penguins)
```


## Video Embedding (Dual HTML/LaTeX)

The course uses **paired chunks** — one for HTML output, one for LaTeX/PDF. This ensures videos render correctly in both formats.

```{r chunkref-video-latex, echo=FALSE, eval = knitr::is_latex_output()}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ VIDEO EMBED — LaTeX/PDF VERSION                                 │
# │ Purpose: Embed a clickable YouTube thumbnail in PDF output.     │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE, eval = knitr::is_latex_output()           │
# │ Notes: embed_youtube_alt() is defined in common.R. It creates  │
# │        a static thumbnail with a hyperlink in non-HTML formats.│
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
embed_youtube_alt("VIDEO_ID_HERE")
```
```{r chunkref-video-html, echo=FALSE, eval = knitr::is_html_output()}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ VIDEO EMBED — HTML VERSION                                      │
# │ Purpose: Embed a playable YouTube iframe in HTML output.        │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE, eval = knitr::is_html_output()            │
# │ Notes: Uses vembedr's embed_url() for responsive iframes.     │
# │        The pipe to use_align("center") centers the video.      │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID_HERE" %>%
  embed_url() %>%
  use_align("center")
```

### Simplified Video Embed (HTML-only chapters)

If your chapter is HTML-only, you can use a single combined chunk:

```{r chunkref-video-simple, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ VIDEO EMBED — SIMPLIFIED (HTML-only)                            │
# │ Purpose: Embed video with automatic format detection.           │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE                                             │
# │ Notes: Uses if/else to pick the right format in one chunk.     │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID_HERE" %>%
    embed_url() %>%
    use_align("center")
} else if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
  embed_youtube_alt("VIDEO_ID_HERE")
}
```


## Slide Embedding

```{r chunkref-slides-from-csv, echo=FALSE, error=TRUE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ SLIDE EMBED — FROM METADATA CSV                                 │
# │ Purpose: Embed slides using URL from ds4p_urls.csv.            │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE, error=TRUE                                 │
# │ Notes: slide_url() is defined in common.R. It looks up the URL │
# │        from the CSV by slide deck name. The optional third arg │
# │        jumps to a specific slide (e.g., "#4").                 │
# │        error=TRUE prevents build failure if URL is missing.    │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
slide_url(ds4p_urls, "d01_welcome") %>%
  include_url(height = "400px")

# Jump to a specific slide:
slide_url(ds4p_urls, "d01_welcome", "#4") %>%
  include_url(height = "400px")
```

```{r chunkref-slides-direct-url, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ SLIDE EMBED — DIRECT URL                                        │
# │ Purpose: Embed slides using a hardcoded URL.                    │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE                                             │
# │ Notes: Use when the slide URL isn't in the metadata CSV.       │
# │        include_url() creates an iframe.                        │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
"https://datascience4psych.github.io/slides/d01_welcome/d01_welcome.html" %>%
  include_url(height = "400px")
```


## Tweet Embedding

```{r chunkref-tweet, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ TWEET EMBED                                                     │
# │ Purpose: Safely embed a tweet (graceful failure if unavailable).│
# │ Options: echo=FALSE                                             │
# │ Notes: try_include_tweet() is defined in common.R. It wraps    │
# │        tweetrmd::include_tweet() in a tryCatch so the build   │
# │        doesn't fail if the tweet is deleted or API is down.    │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
"https://twitter.com/hadleywickham/status/1363920786416562179" %>%
  try_include_tweet()
```


## Image/Graphic Display

```{r chunkref-image-basic, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ IMAGE — BASIC                                                    │
# │ Purpose: Display a static image from the img/ directory.        │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE                                             │
# │ Notes: include_graphics() is preferred over markdown ![](). It │
# │        respects knitr figure options and works in all formats.  │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
knitr::include_graphics("img/example-image.png")
```

```{r chunkref-image-margin, fig.margin=TRUE, echo=FALSE, fig.width=3, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ IMAGE — MARGIN FIGURE                                            │
# │ Purpose: Display a small image in the page margin (tufte-style).│
# │ Options: fig.margin=TRUE, echo=FALSE, fig.width=3              │
# │ Notes: Only works in tufte/bookdown formats that support margin │
# │        content. Falls back to inline in standard HTML.         │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
knitr::include_graphics("img/example-image.png")
```

```{r chunkref-image-fullwidth, fig.fullwidth=TRUE, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ IMAGE — FULL WIDTH                                               │
# │ Purpose: Display an image spanning the full page width.         │
# │ Options: fig.fullwidth=TRUE, echo=FALSE                        │
# │ Notes: Useful for wide figures, comparison images, screenshots. │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
knitr::include_graphics("img/wide-example.png")
```

```{r chunkref-image-captioned, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A descriptive caption that appears below the figure.", eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ IMAGE — WITH CAPTION                                             │
# │ Purpose: Display an image with a numbered figure caption.       │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE, fig.cap="..."                              │
# │ Notes: bookdown auto-numbers figures with captions. Use this   │
# │        when you want to cross-reference with \@ref(fig:label). │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
knitr::include_graphics("img/example-image.png")
```

```{r chunkref-image-alt, echo=FALSE, fig.alt="Alt text describing the image for screen readers", eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ IMAGE — WITH ALT TEXT (ACCESSIBILITY)                            │
# │ Purpose: Provide screen-reader-friendly alt text for images.    │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE, fig.alt="..."                              │
# │ Notes: fig.alt adds an alt attribute to the <img> tag. Use for │
# │        accessibility. Different from fig.cap (visual caption). │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
knitr::include_graphics("img/example-image.png")
```

```{r chunkref-image-sized, echo=FALSE, out.width="50%", eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ IMAGE — CUSTOM SIZE (out.width)                                  │
# │ Purpose: Control the display width of an image.                 │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE, out.width="50%"                            │
# │ Notes: out.width controls the CSS/LaTeX display size. Common   │
# │        values: "50%", "80%", "300px". Does not change the     │
# │        actual image resolution.                                │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
knitr::include_graphics("img/example-image.png")
```


## Interactive Tables (DT)

```{r chunkref-dt-table, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ INTERACTIVE TABLE (DT::datatable)                                │
# │ Purpose: Display a searchable, sortable, paginated table.       │
# │ Options: message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE              │
# │ Notes: DT tables only render in HTML output. Common options:   │
# │   - rownames=FALSE: hide row numbers                           │
# │   - class="cell-border stripe": alternating row colors         │
# │   - filter=list(position='top'): column search boxes           │
# │   - pageLength: rows per page (set to nrow() to show all)     │
# │   - paging=FALSE: disable pagination entirely                 │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
penguins %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  DT::datatable(
    rownames = FALSE,
    class = "cell-border stripe",
    filter = list(position = "top"),
    options = list(
      pageLength = 10,
      autoWidth = TRUE,
      bInfo = FALSE,
      paging = TRUE
    )
  )
```


## Shiny App Embedding

````markdown
```{r chunkref-shiny-app, echo=FALSE}`r ''`
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ SHINY APP EMBED                                                  │
# │ Purpose: Embed a live Shiny app in the HTML output.             │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE                                             │
# │ Notes: Only works in HTML output. The app must be deployed to  │
# │        shinyapps.io or similar hosting. include_app() creates  │
# │        an iframe with appropriate sizing.                      │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
knitr::include_app("https://minecr.shinyapps.io/unvotes/")
```
````


## External Page Embedding (include_url)

```{r chunkref-include-url, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ EXTERNAL PAGE EMBED                                              │
# │ Purpose: Embed any webpage in an iframe.                        │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE                                             │
# │ Notes: Used for activity previews, slide decks, compiled HTML. │
# │        height parameter controls iframe height.                │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
"https://datascience4psych.github.io/ae01a_unvotes/unvotes.html" %>%
  include_url(height = "400px")
```


## Data Visualization / Plot Chunks

```{r chunkref-plot-basic, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ PLOT — BASIC (code shown)                                       │
# │ Purpose: Show code AND output for teaching.                     │
# │ Options: echo=TRUE (default), message=FALSE, warning=FALSE    │
# │ Notes: This is the bread-and-butter teaching chunk. Students   │
# │        see both the code and the resulting plot.               │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = flipper_length_mm, y = body_mass_g)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = species), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r chunkref-plot-hidden-code, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ PLOT — HIDDEN CODE (output only)                                │
# │ Purpose: Show a plot without revealing the code that made it.  │
# │ Options: echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE             │
# │ Notes: Used for "here's what your result should look like"     │
# │        previews, or decorative/contextual figures.             │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, fill = species)) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r chunkref-plot-fullwidth, fig.fullwidth=TRUE, echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ PLOT — FULL WIDTH                                                │
# │ Purpose: Display a wide plot spanning the full page.            │
# │ Options: fig.fullwidth=TRUE                                     │
# │ Notes: Good for faceted plots or plots with many groups.       │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, fill = species)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 200) +
  facet_wrap(~species, ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r chunkref-plot-custom-dims, fig.width=8, fig.height=4, echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ PLOT — CUSTOM DIMENSIONS                                         │
# │ Purpose: Control the aspect ratio of a plot.                    │
# │ Options: fig.width=8, fig.height=4                              │
# │ Notes: fig.width/fig.height set the R graphics device size in  │
# │        inches. Wider = better for time series, taller = better │
# │        for vertical comparisons.                               │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm, color = species)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal()
```


## Student Workspace / Exercise Chunks

```{r chunkref-student-workspace, error=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ STUDENT WORKSPACE                                                │
# │ Purpose: Provide a scaffolded code template for students.       │
# │ Options: error=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE  │
# │ Notes: error=TRUE prevents build failure from incomplete code. │
# │        Students fill in the blanks and run the chunk.          │
# │        Use eval=FALSE if the placeholder code would error.    │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
species_counts <- penguins %>%
  1 # replace this with your code
```

```{r chunkref-student-fillin, eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ STUDENT FILL-IN-THE-BLANK                                       │
# │ Purpose: Show a code template with blanks for students to fill.│
# │ Options: eval=FALSE                                             │
# │ Notes: eval=FALSE means the chunk is displayed but not run.   │
# │        Students copy to their own file and complete the code.  │
# │        Use ___ as placeholders for missing parts.             │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
penguins %>%
  group_by(___) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_mass = mean(___),
    sd_mass = sd(___)
  )
```


## Hidden Solutions (ref.label pattern)

This is the key pedagogical pattern. You write the solution once in a hidden chunk, then display it on-demand inside a `<details>` block.

```{r chunkref-solution-hidden, error=TRUE, include=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ HIDDEN SOLUTION CHUNK                                            │
# │ Purpose: Store executable solution code, hidden from students.  │
# │ Options: include=FALSE (hides EVERYTHING — code AND output)    │
# │ Notes: This chunk RUNS during the build (so errors are caught) │
# │        but nothing appears in the rendered output. It is       │
# │        re-displayed via ref.label in a <details> block.       │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
species_summary <- penguins %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_mass = mean(body_mass_g),
    sd_mass = sd(body_mass_g)
  )
```

Then display it inside collapsible details using `ref.label`:

<details>
  <summary>Click to see the solution</summary>
```{r ref.label = "chunkref-solution-hidden", echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE}
```
</details>

````markdown
<!-- The raw markdown for the pattern above looks like this: -->

```{r my-solution, error=TRUE, include=FALSE}`r ''`
# Solution code here — runs but is hidden
result <- penguins %>%
  count(species)
```

<details>
  <summary>Click to see the solution</summary>
```{r ref.label = "my-solution", echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE}`r ''`
```
</details>
````


## Nested Hints (details within details)

<details>
  <summary>Click here to see hints</summary>

<details>
  <summary>Big Picture Hint</summary>
Start by thinking about what function groups rows together...
</details>
.
<details>
  <summary>Step 1 Hint</summary>
Use `group_by(species)` to group the data by species.
</details>
.
<details>
  <summary>Step 2 Hint</summary>
Inside `summarize()`, use `mean()` and `sd()` on the variable of interest.
</details>
</details>

````markdown
<!-- The raw markdown for nested hints: -->

<details>
  <summary>Click here to see hints</summary>

<details>
  <summary>Big Picture Hint</summary>
Start by thinking about what function groups rows together...
</details>
.
<details>
  <summary>Step 1 Hint</summary>
Use `group_by(species)` to group the data by species.
</details>
.
<details>
  <summary>Step 2 Hint</summary>
Inside `summarize()`, use `mean()` and `sd()`.
</details>
</details>
````


## Conditional Evaluation (API-gated chunks)

````markdown
```{r api-setup, include=FALSE}`r ''`
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ API KEY AVAILABILITY CHECK                                       │
# │ Purpose: Define booleans for conditional chunk evaluation.      │
# │ Options: include=FALSE                                          │
# │ Notes: Checks environment variables for API keys. Downstream   │
# │        chunks use eval=[variable] to skip if key is missing.   │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
ebirdkey_available <- Sys.getenv("EBIRD_API_KEY") != ""
geonamesUsername_available <- Sys.getenv("GEONAMES_USERNAME") != ""
```

```{r api-demo, eval = ebirdkey_available, error=TRUE, cache=TRUE}`r ''`
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ API-GATED CHUNK                                                  │
# │ Purpose: Run code only when an API key is available.            │
# │ Options: eval=[boolean_variable], error=TRUE, cache=TRUE       │
# │ Notes: eval takes a variable name (no quotes). error=TRUE      │
# │        prevents build failure. cache=TRUE avoids re-calling    │
# │        the API on every build.                                 │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
library(rebird)
ebirdgeo(species = "Haliaeetus leucocephalus", lat = 42, lng = -76)
```
````


## Cached Chunks (Expensive Computations)

````markdown
```{r expensive-computation, cache=TRUE, message=FALSE}`r ''`
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ CACHED CHUNK                                                     │
# │ Purpose: Cache results of slow computations.                    │
# │ Options: cache=TRUE                                             │
# │ Notes: Results are stored in a cache directory and reused on   │
# │        subsequent builds if the code hasn't changed. Use for:  │
# │        - API calls                                             │
# │        - Large data processing                                 │
# │        - Model fitting                                         │
# │        Be careful: cache invalidation can be tricky.           │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
big_result <- penguins %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(species, island, sex) %>%
  summarize(across(where(is.numeric), list(mean = mean, sd = sd)))
```
````


## Raw Output (results='asis')

````markdown
```{r raw-output, results='asis', echo=FALSE}`r ''`
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ RAW MARKDOWN/HTML OUTPUT                                         │
# │ Purpose: Output raw markdown or HTML that bookdown renders.     │
# │ Options: results='asis', echo=FALSE                             │
# │ Notes: Normally, R output is wrapped in a code block. With     │
# │        results='asis', cat() output is treated as raw markup.  │
# │        Useful for dynamically generated markdown tables,       │
# │        HTML elements, or conditional content.                  │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
cat("### This heading was generated by R code\n\n")
cat("And this paragraph too. You can use **markdown** here.\n")
```
````


## Child Document Includes

```{r chunkref-child-courselinks, child="includes/courselinks.md", eval=FALSE}
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ CHILD DOCUMENT INCLUDE                                           │
# │ Purpose: Include shared content from another file.              │
# │ Options: child="path/to/file.md"                                │
# │ Notes: The child file is read and rendered inline as if its    │
# │        content were pasted at this location. Used for:         │
# │        - courselinks.md (footer links on every page)           │
# │        - links.md (reference-style link definitions)           │
# │        - Shared content across multiple chapters               │
# │        The chunk body is ignored when child is specified.      │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

````markdown
<!-- Common child includes you'll use: -->

```{r courselinks, child="includes/courselinks.md"}`r ''`
```

```{r links-general, child="includes/links.md"}`r ''`
```
````


## Bibliography Generation

````markdown
```{r generate-package-bib, include = FALSE}`r ''`
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ BIBLIOGRAPHY GENERATION                                          │
# │ Purpose: Auto-generate .bib entries for loaded R packages.      │
# │ Options: include = FALSE                                        │
# │ Notes: Creates packages.bib with citation info for all packages│
# │        used in the session. Typically in front matter only.    │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
knitr::write_bib(
  c(.packages(), "bookdown", "knitr", "rmarkdown"),
  "packages.bib"
)
```
````


## Session Info / Colophon

````markdown
```{r session-info, message=FALSE, error=TRUE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}`r ''`
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ SESSION INFO                                                     │
# │ Purpose: Display R version and package versions for             │
# │          reproducibility documentation.                        │
# │ Options: message=FALSE, error=TRUE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE │
# │ Notes: error=TRUE because devtools::session_info() can fail   │
# │        in some build environments. Typically in back matter.   │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
all_pkgs <- renv::dependencies(path = "DESCRIPTION") %>%
  pull(Package)
session <- devtools::session_info(pkgs = all_pkgs)
session$platform
```
````


## Inline R Code

Inline code lets you weave computed values into prose text:

````markdown
The dataset contains `r knitr::inline_expr("nrow(penguins)")` penguins
from `r knitr::inline_expr("n_distinct(penguins$species)")` species.
````

Renders as: The dataset contains `r nrow(penguins)` penguins from `r n_distinct(penguins$species)` species.


## Emoji Commit Checkpoint Pattern

This isn't a chunk type, but a recurring markdown pattern used in labs between exercise sections:

````markdown
🧶 ✅ ⬆️ Knit, commit, and push your changes to GitHub with the
commit message "Completed Exercise 1". Make sure to commit and push
all changed files so that your Git pane is cleared up afterwards.
````


## Quick Reference: Option Cheat Sheet

| Option | Values | Purpose |
|--------|--------|---------|
| `echo` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` | Show/hide the code |
| `eval` | `TRUE`/`FALSE`/variable | Run/skip the code |
| `include` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` | Show/hide everything (code + output) |
| `message` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` | Show/hide messages (e.g., package loading) |
| `warning` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` | Show/hide warnings |
| `error` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` | `TRUE` = continue build on error; `FALSE` = stop |
| `cache` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` | Cache results for faster rebuilds |
| `results` | `'markup'`/`'asis'`/`'hide'` | How to format output |
| `fig.width` | number (inches) | R device width |
| `fig.height` | number (inches) | R device height |
| `fig.fullwidth` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` | Span full page width |
| `fig.margin` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` | Place in margin (tufte) |
| `fig.cap` | string | Numbered figure caption |
| `fig.alt` | string | Alt text for accessibility |
| `out.width` | `"50%"`, `"300px"` | CSS display width |
| `child` | file path | Include another document |
| `ref.label` | chunk label | Re-display another chunk's code |


```{r courselinks, child="includes/courselinks.md"}
```
